name: knowledge
context: Knowledge checks - aliases, body depth, sections, links, and agent-learn coverage
info: This sub-skill asks whether a manifest will actually teach well. It checks for realistic aliases, usable long-form content, sections worth addressing directly, and graph edges that connect the node to the rest of the tree.
triggers: why is my node not useful in agent learn
body:
# Knowledge Validation

Knowledge validation asks whether the node will actually teach. Does it have aliases a person would search for? Does it have a body or sections worth learning from? Are there links that connect it into the graph and lead to an actionable next step?

That is the difference between a bare manifest and a node that works well with [[dojo/knowledge]] and [[dojo/api/query#agent-workflows]].

sections.title: Discovery signals
sections.body: Check `aliases`, `triggers`, and `tags` against the words a user would really type. Search quality depends on human phrasing, not just canonical terms.

sections.title: Long-form content
sections.body: A context-heavy node should have a meaningful `body`, addressable `sections`, or both. Empty knowledge fields make `/v1/learn` and `/v1/agent/learn` much less useful.

sections.title: Actionability
sections.body: Good knowledge nodes do not stop at explanation. They point toward the next useful thing to do, ideally with at least one link to a `skill` or `sub` node and at least one section-target link that keeps the reading path precise. This is what turns a wiki note into an agent workflow.

sections.title: Graph connections
sections.body: Use `links` for clear next steps and `related` for softer conceptual edges. If a node never points anywhere, it becomes a dead end in the knowledge graph. Validation should also warn when those links point to missing nodes or missing sections.

links.context: The model this sub-skill is trying to protect
Authoring workflow that should include knowledge checks
Read-side client that benefits from stronger knowledge content
Run a local smoke test after knowledge validation

related.note: This sub-skill applies the authoring patterns described in the knowledge guide

scripts.name: Knowledge Only Validation
scripts.description: Check discovery and long-form knowledge quality

author: dojo-community
